title: Public redacted version of the Decision on the CLRV request for extension of time and extension of page limit and scheduling a hearing before the Chamber
text:
ICC-02/05-01/20-1317-Red 02-04-2026 1/6
Original: English No. ICC-02/05-01/20
Date: 2 April 2026
TRIAL CHAMBER I
Before: Judge Joanna Korner, Presiding Judge
Judge Reine Alapini-Gansou
Judge Althea Violet Alexis-Windsor
SITUATION IN DARFUR, SUDAN
IN THE CASE OF
THE PROSECUTOR v. ALI MUHAMMAD ALI ABD-AL-RAHMAN (‘ALI
KUSHAYB’)
Public redacted version of the
Decision on the CLRV request for extension of time and extension of page limit
and scheduling a hearing before the Chamber
No: ICC-02/05-01/20 1/6 2 April 2026

ICC-02/05-01/20-1317-Red 02-04-2026 2/6

No: ICC-02/05-01/20 2/6 2 April 2026

ICC-02/05-01/20-1317-Red 02-04-2026 3/6
I. PROCEDURAL HISTORY
1. On 6 October 2025, Trial Chamber I (the ‘Chamber’) delivered its judgment pursuant to
Article 74 of the Rome Statute (the ‘Statute’), convicting Ali Muhammad Ali Abd-Al-Rahman
of 27 counts of crimes against humanity and war crimes (the ‘Trial Judgment’).1
2. On 9 December 2025, the Chamber sentenced Abd-Al-Rahman to a joint sentence of 20
years for the crimes of which he was convicted.2
3. On the same day, the Chamber ordered the parties to file submissions on reparations by
4 May 2026 and responses thereto by 4 June 2026.3
4. On 11 February 2026, having received requests from several organisations to file
observations pursuant to Rule 103(1) of the Rules of Procedure and Evidence (the ‘Rules’), the
Chamber granted leave to four organisations to file amici curiae submissions by 4 May 2026.4
5. On 30 March 2026, the common legal representative for victims (the ‘CLRV’), filed a
request seeking an extension of time limit and extension of page limit to file her submissions
(the ‘Request’).5
6. On 31 March 2026, the Prosecution and the Defence responded that they do not object to
the Request and submit that the same deadline should apply to them, if the extension of time
sought is granted.6
7. On 1 April 2026, the Registry transmitted its observations, [REDACTED].7
1 Trial Judgment, ICC-02/05-01/20-1240.
2 Sentencing Judgment, ICC-02/05-01/20-1281-Conf (with one confidential annex). Public redacted versions were
notified on the same day, ICC-02/05-01/20-1281-Red and ICC-02/05-01/20-1281-Anx-Red.
3 Order for submissions on reparations, ICC-02/05-01/20-1282 (hereinafter: ‘Order’).
4 Decision on requests for leave to file amicus curiae submissions on reparations, ICC-02/05-01/20-1308-Red.
5 CLRV Request for a limited extension of time to submit her Observations on Reparations and for a limited
extension of pages, ICC-02/05-01/20-1316-Conf.
6 Email from the Prosecution at 12:53; Email from the Defence at 13:54.
7 Email from the Registry at 17:32. [REDACTED].
No: ICC-02/05-01/20 3/6 2 April 2026

ICC-02/05-01/20-1317-Red 02-04-2026 4/6
II. SUBMISSIONS AND ANALYSIS
A. Extension of time limit
8. The CLRV seeks an extension of time limit of one month (from 4 May 2026 to 4 June
2026).8 She submits that such an extension is necessary due to a [REDACTED] to consult with
victims for reasons outside her control that could not be anticipated.9 She also submits that
currently there are concurrent responses due in the pending appeals against the conviction and
sentencing decisions. 10 The CLRV states that such an extension is necessary for her to
incorporate the results [REDACTED] into the written observations.11
9. The CLRV also notes that, after having consulted with the Defence, the Prosecution, the
Victims Participation and Reparations Section (the ‘VPRS’) and the TFV, neither objected to
the CLRV’s request; however, they expressed to the CLRV that they would welcome that the
new deadline applies equally to all.12 This was reiterated by the parties, the Registry in their
responses to the Request.13
10. [REDACTED],14 in light of the circumstances described by the CLRV and the Registry,
the Chamber finds that good cause is shown to warrant an extension of time pursuant to
Regulation 35(2) of the Regulations of the Court (the ‘Regulations’).
11. Noting that the amicus curiae observations will be filed on the original deadline of 4 May
2026, the submissions on reparations should already address any issues arising from the amicus
curiae observations.
8 Request, para. 1.
9 Request, paras 1, 8-9, 11.
10 Request, paras 3, 7.
11 Request, para. 10.
12 Request, para. 13.
13 Email from the Prosecution on 31 March 2026 at 12:53; Email from the Defence on 31 March 2026 at 13:54;
Email from the Registry on 1 April 2026 at 17:32.
14 See details provided in the Email from the Registry on 1 April 2026 at 17:32.
No: ICC-02/05-01/20 4/6 2 April 2026

ICC-02/05-01/20-1317-Red 02-04-2026 5/6
B. Extension of page limit
12. The CLRV also requests that the page limit be extended from 50 to 70 pages.15 She
submits this is justified considering the substantial body of information she has gathered so
far.16
13. The Chamber notes that the parties do not object to the extension of page limit sought.17
Considering that in their observations, the parties and participants should already respond to
the amicus curiae observations, the Chamber hereby extends the page limit, pursuant to
Regulation 37 of the Regulations, from 50 to 60 pages for the parties, as well as the Registry
and the TFV. Nonetheless, the Chamber emphasises, as noted by the CLRV, 18 that all
submissions must be concrete, practical and tailored to the specific circumstances of the victims
in this case.
C. Scheduling of a hearing
14. The Chamber notes that in previous reparations proceedings, a hearing has been
scheduled for the Chamber to pose questions to the parties and participants on issues arising
from their written observations, as well as to hear final observations on reparations.
15. The Chamber will therefore hold a public hearing on 8 September 2026 at 9.30.19 An
agenda for the hearing will be issued in due course.
16. Should any of the participating amici curiae wish to attend the hearing, they are to file a
request before the Chamber by 4 May 2026, when their submissions are due.
15 Request, para. 14.
16 Request, para. 15.
17 Email from the Prosecution at 12:53; Email from the Defence at 13:54.
18 Request, para. 15.
19 The Chamber may authorise private sessions, when justified, for very discrete instances where the parties and
the participants must refer to confidential information.
No: ICC-02/05-01/20 5/6 2 April 2026

ICC-02/05-01/20-1317-Red 02-04-2026 6/6
FOR THE FOREGOING REASONS, THE CHAMBER HEREBY
GRANTS the Request;
ORDERS the CLRV, the Defence, the Prosecution, the Registry and the TFV to file their
submissions by 4 June 2026;
EXTENDS the page limit for the above submissions to 60 pages;
ORDERS the CLRV, the Defence, the Prosecution, the Registry and the TFV to file responses
to the aforesaid submissions by 3 July 2026; and
SCHEDULES a hearing on 8 September 2026.
________________________________
Judge Joanna Korner
Presiding Judge
_______________________________ ________________________________
Judge Reine Alapini-Gansou Judge Althea Violet Alexis-Windsor
Dated this 2 April 2026
At The Hague, The Netherlands
No: ICC-02/05-01/20 6/6 2 April 2026
